Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. El Amargal: camino hasta cuchilla de Arusí. With Armancio as guide. Light rain and super quiet overall. After walking up the trail a few hundred meters we turned back and I ended the list a little short of the El Amargal buildings when it was getting dark.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Walked east from the town of Termales, reached the "termales spa del pacifico", and then took the Camino Cuatro Encantos. We walked only a few hundred meters along the camino and then turned back. Scouting for Manacus. With Sebastian, Maira, and guides. Ebird track for this list is WAY off due to user error--accidentally continued a list from a previous site. Have since fixed error, except for lingering eBird track.
Manacus2022_

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. After setting up nets. With Salma, Mori, and Callito.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota [AEM edit: from Medellin I think] and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the stream to an impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in his birthday suit; Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest.
Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022.
Manacus2022_
Manacus2022_

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. With Armancio as guide, we started at the El Amargal clearing then walked the Camino hasta Cuchilla de Arusí. Ending at clearing near the cuchilla (ridgetop) where we ate breakfast and hung out for a while. Good lighting and open views there for some canopy birds.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota [AEM edit: from Medellin I think] and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the stream to an impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in his birthday suit; Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. El Amargal: camino hasta cuchilla de Arusí. With Armancio as guide. Light rain and super quiet overall. After walking up the trail a few hundred meters we turned back and I ended the list a little short of the El Amargal buildings when it was getting dark.
